clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL), anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL), and progressive AKI (Stage 1 to 3) meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) persists across three measurements. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) suggests possible hepatic involvement. AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) was excluded as outlier but preceded by Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL).

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Sepsis-3: suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rise from 2.1 to 8.9 mg/dL exceeds KDIGO Stage 3 threshold
prioritized_risks.threshold: KDIGO AKI: creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coag score 3 (normal: 150-400 K/uL)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor use/ MAP status unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Septic shock: vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 + lactate >2 mmol/L post-fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis bundle: broad-spectrum antibiotics, fluid resuscitation, lactate clearance monitoring
Order urgent renal replacement therapy consultation for AKI Stage 3
Check coagulation panel (INR, PT/TT) given thrombocytopenia
Obtain blood cultures and infectious workup
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding
doctor_handoff: Review cultures/imaging for infection source, assess need for vasopressors, and evaluate for multi-organ failure progression

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.

family_communication.english_summary: In the last 12 hours, your loved one has shown signs of a severe infection (sepsis) that is affecting multiple organs. Their blood tests show high lactate (4.2 mmol/L), low platelets (88-92 K/uL), and worsening kidney function (AKI Stage 3 with creatinine 8.9 mg/dL, though a recent creatinine jump may need rechecking). The care team has started urgent treatment: antibiotics for the infection, fluids to support blood pressure, and is preparing for kidney dialysis. They are also checking for the infection's source and monitoring for bleeding risks due to low platelets. The next steps include confirming the infection cause, managing kidney support, and closely watching for complications like liver involvement.
family_communication.regional_summary: पिछले 12 घंटों में आपके प्रिय व्यक्ति के शरीर में गंभीर संक्रमण (सिप्सिस) के लक्षण हैं जो कई अंगों को प्रभावित कर रहा है। उनके रक्त परीक्षण में लैक्टेट बढ़ा (4.2 mmol/L), प्लेटलेट्स कम (88-92 K/uL), और गुर्दे का काम करना घट गया है (AKI चरण 3, क्रिएटिनीन 8.9 mg/dL, हालांकि हालिया क्रिएटिनीन में बढ़ोतरी की पुष्टि की जरूरत है)। चिकित्सा टीम ने त्वरित उपचार शुरू कर दिया है: संक्रमण के लिए एंटीबायोटिक्स, रक्तचाप को समर्थन के लिए तरल, और गुर्दा डायलिसिस की तैयारी। वे संक्रमण के स्रोत की जांच कर रहे हैं और कम प्लेटलेट्स के कारण रक्तस्राव के जोखिम की निगरानी कर रहे हैं। अगले कदम में संक्रमण के कारण की पुष्टि, गुर्दा समर्थन का प्रबंधन, और जिगर की जांच शामिल है।
